skill.name: finishing-a-development-branch
skill.description: Use when implementation is complete, all tests pass, and you need to decide how to integrate the work - guides completion of development work by presenting structured options for merge, PR, or cleanup
skill.summary: Guide for completing development branches by verifying tests, presenting merge/PR/cleanup options, and executing the chosen workflow with proper cleanup
skill.author: ZhanlinCui

security_audit.summary: All 27 static findings are false positives. The skill contains documentation (markdown instructions) showing example git commands, not actual executable code with command injection risks. The YAML frontmatter flagged as 'weak cryptographic algorithm' is standard skill metadata. The 'external_commands' patterns are markdown code blocks showing example commands users should run, not Ruby/shell backtick execution.

high_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm (FALSE POSITIVE)
high_findings.description: Static scanner flagged YAML frontmatter 'name:' field as cryptographic. This is standard skill metadata, not cryptographic code.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: YAML frontmatter contains skill name/description, not cryptographic algorithms

medium_findings.title: External Commands in Documentation (FALSE POSITIVE)
medium_findings.description: 23 instances flagged as 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' are markdown code blocks containing example shell commands for documentation. The skill provides instructions to Claude on what git commands to run, not actual code execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: These are markdown fenced code blocks (```bash) showing example commands, not backtick execution. Standard documentation pattern for skill workflows.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Access in Documentation (FALSE POSITIVE)
low_findings.description: References to /dev/null are in example command strings showing stderr redirection, not actual device file access
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: /dev/null in '2>/dev/null' is a standard shell redirection example in documentation

content.value_statement: This skill helps Claude guide developers through finishing a feature branch by verifying tests pass, presenting clear merge/PR/cleanup options, and executing the chosen workflow safely.
actual_capabilities: Verify tests pass before presenting completion options
Present 4 structured options: merge locally, create PR, keep branch, or discard
Execute chosen workflow with proper git commands
Clean up git worktrees after merge or discard operations
Require explicit confirmation before discarding work
Determine base branch and feature branch names automatically
limitations: Does not run the actual test suite - user must execute tests manually
Requires gh CLI installed for creating pull requests
Does not handle merge conflicts - user must resolve them
Does not support non-standard git hosting platforms beyond GitHub

use_cases.title: Complete a Feature Branch
use_cases.description: After implementing a feature, use this skill to cleanly merge the branch, create a PR, or safely discard the work
use_cases.target_user: Software developers working on feature branches

use_cases.title: Agent Workflow Integration
use_cases.description: Called as final step in subagent-driven-development or executing-plans skills after all tasks complete

use_cases.title: Branch Cleanup After Code Review
use_cases.description: Use after PR approval to merge and clean up the feature branch from local and remote
use_cases.target_user: Developers preparing code for merge after review

prompt_templates.title: Complete My Feature Branch
prompt_templates.prompt: I'm done implementing my feature. Can you help me finish this development branch? Run tests first, then give me options for how to proceed.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has completed feature work and wants to merge or clean up

prompt_templates.title: Merge and Cleanup
prompt_templates.prompt: All tests pass. Please merge this branch into main and clean up the worktree.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to merge locally and cleanup

prompt_templates.title: Create Pull Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Tests are green. Please push this branch and create a pull request for review.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to create a PR for code review

prompt_templates.title: Discard Work Safely
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to discard this work. Please confirm before deleting the branch.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to safely discard experimental work

output_examples.input: Run the skill after tests pass
output_examples.output:
Implementation complete. What would you like to do?

1. Merge back to main locally
2. Push and create a Pull Request
3. Keep the branch as-is (I'll handle it later)
4. Discard this work

Which option?

Switched to 'main'.
Pulled latest changes.
Merged 'feature-branch'.
Tests pass on merged result.
Deleted branch 'feature-branch'.
Worktree cleaned up. Branch complete.

best_practices: Always verify tests pass before presenting options to prevent merging broken code
Present exactly 4 options without additional explanation to keep user decision clear
Require typed 'discard' confirmation before deleting work to prevent accidental loss
Only clean up worktrees for merge and discard options, not for PR creation
anti_patterns: Skipping test verification and proceeding directly to merge or PR
Presenting open-ended questions instead of structured 4-option choice
Automatically cleaning up worktrees for all options (breaks Option 2 and 3)
Allowing discard without explicit typed confirmation

faq.question: Does this skill run tests automatically?
faq.answer: No. The skill instructs Claude to verify tests pass but the user must run the actual test command. Claude presents the example commands but does not execute them.

faq.question: What happens if tests fail?
faq.answer: The skill stops and reports the test failures. It does not proceed to merge or PR options until tests pass.

faq.question: Can I use this with GitLab or Bitbucket?
faq.answer: This skill is designed for GitHub with gh CLI. GitLab and Bitbucket have different PR creation workflows not covered.

faq.question: How does the skill determine the base branch?
faq.answer: It tries common base branches (main, master) using git merge-base. If unclear, it asks the user to confirm.

faq.question: What safety measures exist for discard?
faq.answer: Before discarding, the skill shows exactly what will be deleted (branch, commits, worktree) and requires the user to type 'discard' to confirm.

faq.question: Does this skill handle merge conflicts?
faq.answer: No. The skill does not attempt to resolve merge conflicts. If conflicts occur, it reports them and stops for user intervention.
